title: Preparation of Spike-in Aliquots of Murine Hepatitis Virus (MHV)
author: Melissa Pitton
materials: Aliquot 510 µL into each prepared Eppendorf tube.
Extract the MHV spike-in aliquot in triplicate using the QiaAmp Viral RNA Minikit (Qiagen) according to manufacturer's instructions.
For each sample, place a QIAamp Mini spin column (Qiagen) in a 2 mL collection tube.
Carefully pipet 630 µL of the sample to the QIAamp Mini spin column (Qiagen).

steps.title: Clean the sterile bench with Ethanol...
steps.instruction: Clean the sterile bench with Ethanol 70% or other appropriate disinfectant and turn on heat block to 65°C.

steps.title: Prepare the 1
steps.instruction: Prepare the 1.5 mL tubes for the desired number of aliquots. Switch on the UV light for 20 minutes, if available, to disinfect surfaces.

steps.title: Heat four 50 µL aliquots of...
steps.instruction: Heat four 50 µL aliquots of cultivated MHV on a heat block until thawed.

steps.title: In the meantime, prepare 100 mL...
steps.instruction: In the meantime, prepare 100 mL 1x PBS solution, by adding 10 mL of 10x PBS to 90 mL of nuclease-free water in a 250 mL bottle (Corning).

steps.title: Dilute the cultivated MHV stock 1:500...
steps.instruction: Dilute the cultivated MHV stock 1:500 by adding the 200 µL cultivated MHV to the 100 mL 1x PBS solution.

steps.title: Invent the 250 mL bottle (Corning)...
steps.instruction: Invent the 250 mL bottle (Corning) a few times and vortex intermittently for approximately 10-20 seconds. Caution: Do not vortex the solution for too long as this can affect the virus.

steps.title: Place the aliquots in the boxes and store at -80°C
steps.instruction: Place the aliquots in the boxes and store at -80°C.

steps.title: Prepare the heat block at 65°C
steps.instruction: Prepare the heat block at 65°C.

steps.title: Take an MHV spike-in aliquot from...
steps.instruction: Take an MHV spike-in aliquot from the -80°C and thaw on the heat block at 65°C until thawed. The aliquot can be removed when only a small piece of ice remains in the aliquot to prevent overheating. Caution: Aliquots should always be thawed on the heat block and not with hands or at room temperature, to prevent degradation of the virus due to slow thawing. Observe the sample thawing to prevent overheating, which will reduce the portion of virus.

steps.title: Pipet 560 µL AVL buffer, 5
steps.instruction: Pipet 560 µL AVL buffer, 5.6 µL carrier RNA and 140 µL MHV spike-in together in a 2 mL tube. Vortex the tubes.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (15 - 25°C) for 10 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (15 - 25°C) for 10 min.

steps.title: Spin the tube on a table...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube on a table top centrifuge quickly to remove drops from the inside of the lid.

steps.title: Add 560 µL ethanol (96%-100%) to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 560 µL ethanol (96%-100%) to each sample. Mix by vortexing.

steps.title: Spin the tube on a table...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube on a table top centrifuge quickly to remove drops from the inside of the lid.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 6’000 x g (8’000 rpm) for 1 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 6’000 x g (8’000 rpm) for 1 min.

steps.title: Keep the spin column and discard...
steps.instruction: Keep the spin column and discard the collection tube containing the filtrate. Place the spin column into a new collection tube.

steps.title: Repeat steps 20-22
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 20-22.

steps.title: Add 500 µL buffer AW1 (Qiagen)
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL buffer AW1 (Qiagen). Centrifuge at 6’000 x g (8’000 rpm) for 1 min.

steps.title: Keep the spin column and discard...
steps.instruction: Keep the spin column and discard the collection tube containing the filtrate. Place the spin column into a new collection tube.

steps.title: Add 500 µL buffer AW2 (Qiagen)
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL buffer AW2 (Qiagen). Centrifuge at 20’000 x g (14’000 rpm) or max. speed of the centrifuge for 3 min.

steps.title: Keep the spin column and discard...
steps.instruction: Keep the spin column and discard the collection tube containing the filtrate. Place the spin column into a new collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge again at 20’000 x g (14’000 rpm) or max
steps.instruction: Centrifuge again at 20’000 x g (14’000 rpm) or max. speed of the centrifuge for 1 min.

steps.title: Place the spin column into a clean, labelled 1
steps.instruction: Place the spin column into a clean, labelled 1.5 mL plastic tube.

steps.title: Open the column and add 40...
steps.instruction: Open the column and add 40 µL of AVE (Qiagen) into the middle of the column.

steps.title: Incubate the spin column for 2...
steps.instruction: Incubate the spin column for 2 min at room temperature and centrifuge then at 6’000 x g (8’000 rpm) for 1 min.

steps.title: Repeat step 30-31, such that the...
steps.instruction: Repeat step 30-31, such that the total extract volume is 80 µL.

steps.title: After extraction, dilute the final extract 10x, 100x, 1000x
steps.instruction: After extraction, dilute the final extract 10x, 100x, 1000x

steps.title: Quantify MHV in the dilutions in...
steps.instruction: Quantify MHV in the dilutions in duplicate by using an appropriate assay, such as the Six-plex Digital PCR targeting SARS-CoV-2 (N1 and N2), MHV, IAV, IBV, and RSV as applied in https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5jyl8xk68v2w/v .

steps.title: This protocol specifically describes the preparation...
steps.instruction: This protocol specifically describes the preparation of the spike-in aliquots of Murine Hepatitis Virus (MHV), used during the total nucleic acid extraction from wastewater as a control to identify sample cross-contamination and estimate proportion of virus recovery through processing.

steps.title: The goal of this protocol is...
steps.instruction: The goal of this protocol is to prepare a stock of MHV spike-in aliquots, extract the aliquots in triplicate using the QiaAmp Viral RNA MiniKit, and then estimate the amount to spike in wastewater samples.

steps.title: Estimate the appropriate volume of MHV...
steps.instruction: Estimate the appropriate volume of MHV to add to each sample (in μL). In our laboratory, we targetat least 400,000 genome copy equivalents: (400,000 genome copy equivalents / (digital PCR output in gc/μL * template volume (i.e. 5 μL) * eluate volume (80μL) / extracted volume of MHV (140μL)))

steps.title: Estimate the amount of the virus...
steps.instruction: Estimate the amount of the virus to spike-in to the sample: (rounded volume * (digital PCR output in gc/μL * template volume (i.e. 5 μL) * eluate volume (80μL) / extracted volume of MHV (140μL)))
